blockingIssues.description: Interrupted runs still serialize caller-owned injected sandbox sessions into durable RunState, allowing caller-managed workspaces to be captured and later resumed through runner-managed state.

Serialized local sandbox state stores only workspace path plus manifest metadata, so runner-owned workspaces do not survive a real teardown/resume boundary after the original temp directory is cleaned up.
Local snapshot fingerprinting and restore ignore symlinks, so symlink-only drift can be missed and restores can recreate incomplete workspaces.

appliedUpdates.description: Documented in `.factory/library/architecture.md` that runner-owned local sandbox sessions are temp-dir backed and durable resume state must preserve workspace contents, not only path/manifest metadata.
